dossier.overview:
An `.owmap` file is the binary overworld map format (v1, magic `OWM1`) shared between the **Operations Desk Map Editor** and the Pokémon Resort C++ terrain loader. It combines a fast binary header, a UTF-8 metadata JSON blob (spawn, camera, NPC placements, 3D model hooks), and three packed terrain layers: height, special ramps, and collision bits.

Maps are authored in the admin **Map Editor**, saved to `pokemon-resort/assets/overworld/maps/*.owmap`, and loaded at runtime by `loadOwmapScene`. Terrain meshes may come upstream from **Island Dreamforge** exports; character sprites on the map reference `.charbin` packages (see charbin-schema).

Canonical encode/decode: `pokemon-resort-page/tools/admin/lib/owmap-format.mjs`. In-repo reference: `pokemon-resort/docs/gameplay/owmap_format.md`.

sections.title: Where .owmap fits
sections.summary: Dreamforge terrain → Map Editor bake → game runtime.

blocks.body: Operations Desk → **Map Editor** (`npm run admin`, port 9477). Left: map file list. Center: unified paint grid (height tint + ramp glyphs + collision overlay + spawn marker). Right: brush tools, ramp presets, and metadata fields. Default maps folder: `pokemon-resort/assets/overworld/maps/`.
blocks.caption: Map Editor with testing.owmap loaded

blocks.title: Authoring pipeline
blocks.caption: Metadata stays JSON-shaped in the editor; terrain grids pack into binary on save
blocks.source:
flowchart LR
    DF[Island Dreamforge] -->|GLB / textures| ME[Map Editor]
    ME -->|paint height ramps collision| JSON[In-memory map object]
    JSON -->|bakeTerrainSpecials + encodeOwmap| OWM[.owmap file]
    OWM -->|loadOwmapScene| GAME[C++ SceneConfig]
    CB[.charbin packages] -->|characters[] refs| ME

blocks.body:
Runtime default for the 3D test screen: `assets/overworld/maps/testing.owmap` (hardcoded in `Overworld3DTestScreen.cpp`).

The editor keeps full `terrain.{height,special,collision}` grids in memory while you paint. On **Save .owmap**, the server calls `bakeTerrainSpecials()` (resolves editor-only auto ramps) then `encodeOwmap()`, which strips terrain from the JSON header and appends the three binary layers.

Both JSON-era `.map.json` imports and binary `.owmap` files round-trip through the same in-memory map shape via `mapFromJson()` / `decodeOwmap()`.

sections.title: Schema reference
sections.summary: Binary container, terrain semantics, and JSON metadata.

blocks.body: Little-endian, version 1. Row-major indexing for all terrain layers: `index = y * width + x`. The JSON metadata block never contains terrain grids — `encodeOwmap()` calls `stripTerrainForMeta()` before stringify.

blocks.html:
OffsetSizeField
04magic OWM1 (0x4F574D31)
42version u16 = 1
62width u16 (1…256)
82height u16 (1…256)
104tileSize f32 (typically 16)
144metaLen u32
18NmetaJson UTF-8 (N = metaLen)
18+NW×Hheight[] u8 per cell
…W×Hspecial[] u8 per cell
…⌈W×H/8⌉collision bits (1 = blocked)

Collision packing: cell (x,y) → bit index i = y*W + x → byte i >> 3, bit i & 7. Unpacked at runtime to u8 0/1 grids.

blocks.title: File structure
blocks.caption: Reader validates exact payload size — no trailing bytes
blocks.source:
flowchart TD
    H[18-byte header] --> J[metaJson UTF-8]
    J --> HT[height W×H bytes]
    HT --> SP[special W×H bytes]
    SP --> CO[collision ceil W×H/8 bytes]
    CO --> SC[SceneConfig via parseSceneMetadata + grids]

blocks.caption: encodeOwmap — pack header, JSON, and terrain layers
loadOwmapScene — validate magic, dimensions, parse JSON, unpack grids

blocks.html:
LayerStorageRangeMeaning
heightu8 per cell0…255Discrete height level; multiplied by tileSize for world Y
specialu8 per cell0…13Ramp / corner shape (see table below)
collision1 bit per cell0/11 = blocked for pathfinding and player movement

specialLabelOn disk?
0Flatyes
1Auto ramp (editor)no — baked to 2–5 or cleared on save
2Ramp northyes
3Ramp eastyes
4Ramp southyes
5Ramp westyes
6–9Convex corners NE/SE/SW/NWyes
10–13Concave corners NE/SE/SW/NWyes

Auto ramp (1) infers cardinal direction from neighbor height deltas in the editor preview. On save, bakeTerrainSpecials() writes 2–5 or clears to 0. The game loader logs an error if special==1 is still present — always re-export from the Map Editor before shipping.

blocks.body: Ramp brush with preset picker (cardinals 2–5, convex 6–9, concave 10–13, auto 1). Grid cells show ramp shorthand glyphs overlaid on height tint.
blocks.caption: Ramp brush and special presets

blocks.body: Blocked brush paints collision bits (red overlay). Follower pathfinding and player movement treat blocked cells as impassable. Height deltas of ±1 and ramp specials still govern step-up rules separately.
blocks.caption: Collision (blocked) brush
bakeTerrainSpecials — resolve AUTO (1) before encodeOwmap

blocks.body: Metadata is everything except `terrain` grids. Parsed by `SceneMetadataParser.cpp` into `SceneConfig` (`Overworld3DConfig.hpp`). Paths in JSON are relative to the game project root unless absolute.

blocks.html:
SectionKey fieldsNotes
Identityid, name, typetype: exterior or interior
gridenabled, tileSize, width, heightWidth/height also duplicated in binary header
visualmesh, material, textureDirectory, origin[], scale3D terrain mesh hook for renderer
playercharacter, spawnTile[], spawnHeight, facingcharacter → sprite package path (.charbin or legacy .character.json)
camerapresetResolved via config/gameplay/camera/presets.json
lightingpreset, brightness, tint[]Exterior/interior presets
characters[]id, characterId, tile[], facing, movementProfileNPC placements — not runtime party state
models[]id, mesh, material, position[], yawDeg, scaleStatic 3D props on the map

characters[] entry

FieldTypeDescription
idstringMap-local NPC id
characterIdstringReferences a package under assets/characters/
tile[x, y]Grid coordinates
facingstringsouth, west, east, north
movementProfilestringstand_still, random_move, line_move, rotate_in_place
lineDistancenumberSteps for line-move profile

blocks.body:
What does **not** belong in metadata:

• Party / box Pokémon state (use Resort + OpenHome — see compatibility-ontology)
• Per-session follower config (see follower-pokemon / session.json)
• Map-level wild Pokémon spawn tables — not in v1 yet (see overworld_pokemon_architecture_handoff.md in the game repo)

sections.title: Authoring in the Map Editor
sections.summary: Brushes, tools, save path, and 3D preview.

blocks.body: Click **Open 3D** to preview the painted height field with ramp corner geometry. Drag to orbit, right-drag to pan. The preview uses the same corner-height rules as `OverworldMapRenderer.cpp`. Import GLB places terrain meshes referenced in `visual` / `models[]`.
blocks.caption: 3D terrain preview modal

blocks.html:
BrushLayerPurpose
Heightterrain.heightPaint discrete levels 0…255
Rampterrain.specialCardinal, corner, or auto ramps
Blockedterrain.collisionImpassable cells
Spawnplayer.spawnTilePlayer start tile (not a terrain layer)

Tools: Paint, Area, Line, Clear, Fill, Raise, Lower, Eyedropper. Brush sizes 1×1 through 5×5.

blocks.body:
Workflow:

1. `cd pokemon-resort-page && npm run admin` → http://127.0.0.1:9477 → **Map Editor** tab
2. Set **Maps folder** (default `pokemon-resort/assets/overworld/maps`) → **Apply folder** → **Refresh**
3. Load an existing `.owmap` or **New map** → paint terrain → set spawn / metadata
4. **Save .owmap** — server bakes auto ramps, writes binary to the maps folder
5. **Download .owmap** — export without saving (same bake path)
6. **Import .map.json** — legacy JSON maps convert via `mapFromJson()` then save as `.owmap`

Admin API (see `tools/admin/server.mjs`): `POST /api/maps/save`, `GET /api/maps/list`, `POST /api/maps/export-owmap`. Feature flag: `owmap-bake`.

sections.title: Writer vs reader
sections.summary: Same OWM1 v1 layout in JavaScript and C++.

blocks.caption: Paths relative to each repo root.

blocks.body: The Map Editor holds a JSON-shaped map object. Save triggers `bakeTerrainSpecials()` then `encodeOwmap()` in `server.mjs` → `writeMapOwmap()`. Terrain never appears inside `metaJson` on disk.

blocks.caption: decodeOwmap — re-open saved maps in the editor

blocks.body:
`loadSceneConfig()` routes `.owmap` paths to `loadOwmapScene()`. Metadata becomes `SceneConfig`; terrain grids populate `TerrainConfig`. Character packages on the map load separately via `loadCharacterDefinition()` when NPCs spawn.

Shared metadata parser: `SceneMetadataParser.cpp` (also used by legacy JSON scene loader in `JsonOverworldLoader.cpp`).

blocks.caption: parseSceneMetadata — visual, grid, player spawn

sections.title: How to extend the format
sections.summary: Version bumps, new metadata, and loader coordination.

blocks.body:
**Safe extensions (v1):**

• Add optional keys to metadata JSON — update `SceneMetadataParser.cpp` to read them; old maps ignore unknown fields
• Add new `special` tile IDs (14+) — coordinate with `ramp-specials.js`, Map Editor presets, and `OverworldMapRenderer.cpp` corner math
• Add NPC `movementProfile` values — extend `parseMovementProfile()` and editor dropdown

**Breaking changes:**

• Bump `version` u16 in the binary header (currently 1) and teach both `decodeOwmap()` and `OwmapOverworldLoader.cpp` to accept the new version
• Change grid packing (e.g. wider collision flags) — requires coordinated writer/reader change
• Never store terrain inside `metaJson` — keeps editor round-trip and binary size predictable

Update `pokemon-resort/docs/gameplay/owmap_format.md` and this article in the same change.

blocks.title: Extension decision flow
blocks.caption: Prefer optional JSON fields before bumping binary version
blocks.source:
flowchart TD
    Q[Need new map data?] --> A{Terrain per cell?}
    A -->|Yes| B{Fits special layer?}
    B -->|Yes| C[Add special ID + renderer]
    B -->|No| D[Consider v2 grid or metadata tile map]
    A -->|No| E[Optional metadata JSON key]
    E --> F[SceneMetadataParser + editor UI]

blocks.html:
CheckWhere
Magic OWM1JS decoder + C++ loader
Version == 1Both sides
Width/height 1…256C++ loader
Payload size matches W×HTruncation errors if short
Metadata JSON parsesparseSceneMetadata
special != 1 on diskC++ loader error (unbaked auto ramp)
visual + player requiredMetadata parser

blocks.caption: Map Editor captures (Playwright from npm run admin)

• `tools/admin/public/ramp-specials.js` — special tile IDs and bake rules
• `tools/admin/public/map-editor.js` — editor UI and save flow
